activities_projects.title: Quantitative Energy Flow Analysis
steps: Use sample data: 10,000 kJ solar energy → producers capture 1% → calculate at each level using 10% rule
Draw energy pyramid with values
Analyze why top carnivores are few
Discuss human food chain implications (vegetarian vs non-veg)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies quantitative aspect of energy flow

activities_projects.title: Case Study on Ozone Depletion Data
steps: Collect ozone concentration data (pre/post-1987)
Plot graph of ozone levels vs years
Explain CFC role, catalytic destruction equation
Discuss recovery status and challenges
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Links chemistry to environmental data analysis

activities_projects.title: Biomagnification Simulation with Numbers
steps: Assign toxin units: producers = 1 unit, herbivores eat 10 producers → 10 units absorbed
Assume 10% retention per level → calculate up to tertiary consumer
Graph concentration vs trophic level
Relate to mercury/DDT in fish/birds/humans
activities_projects.evaluation: Simulation data, graph, real-world link (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Waste Generation and Decomposition Rate Study
materials: Samples: plastic, paper, fruit peel, cloth
steps: Bury samples in soil pots
Observe weekly for 4-6 weeks (weight loss, appearance)
Record data table
Calculate approximate half-life or % degraded
Discuss factors affecting decomposition

activities_projects.title: Food Web Disruption Impact Analysis
steps: Draw complex local food web (10+ species)
Remove one key species (e.g., predator or pollinator)
Predict cascade effects on other populations
Discuss real examples (e.g., overfishing, wolf reintroduction)

activities_projects.title: Research on Plastic Pollution in Local Area
steps: Survey local water body/soil for plastic waste
Research microplastics entry into food chain
Analyze biomagnification risk to humans
Propose mitigation strategies
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects chapter to current environmental crisis

activities_projects.title: Carbon Cycle and Human Interference Model
steps: Draw detailed carbon cycle (photosynthesis, respiration, combustion)
Highlight excess CO2 from fossil fuels/deforestation
Calculate approximate increase in atmospheric CO2
Link to global warming
activities_projects.evaluation: Model detail, human impact explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate: Sustainable Development vs Economic Growth
activities_projects.objective: Critical thinking on environment vs development
steps: Prepare arguments for both sides
Include examples (e.g., renewable energy vs coal)
Conduct structured debate
Conclude with balanced view

activities_projects.title: Ecological Succession Observation/Project
steps: Study a disturbed area (e.g., abandoned plot, post-construction site)
Document pioneer species to climax community over time (or use secondary data)
Explain stages and role in ecosystem stability

activities_projects.title: Innovative Eco-Project: Zero-Waste Model
steps: Build model of home/school with zero-waste features (compost, rainwater harvest, solar)
Calculate waste reduction potential
Explain integration with chapter concepts
Present feasibility
